symptoms.symptom_name: New wet, gurgling cough with pooled oral secretions

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: New wet cough with pooled secretions in a dysphagic patient lying flat is the classic sign of secretion micro-aspiration.
May herald evolving aspiration pneumonia — cannot be excluded remotely.
Resting tachypnoea supports respiratory involvement; exact rate is clinician-measured, though carers may notice faster breathing.
Mild desaturation supports aspiration/hypoventilation; a clinician-measured value in this note, sealed as answer key.
SpO2 maintained >92% on room air made frank LRTI less likely at the moment of in-person assessment.
Non-specific — may reflect distress, respiratory effort or early dehydration from the interrupted hydration route.

symptoms.symptom_name: Complete mechanical occlusion of the PEG tube (visible solid plug)
symptoms.onset: about 8 hours ago, after thick feed without adequate flushing

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Blocked PEG tube with interrupted anticonvulsant delivery
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A visibly plugged tube through which nothing passes is a mechanical occlusion; carer/video observable.

symptoms.symptom_name: Missed evening anti-epileptic doses (sodium valproate + levetiracetam) for ~8 hours

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Interrupted anticonvulsant cover in an epilepsy-dependent patient is a recognised precipitant of breakthrough seizures.

symptoms.symptom_name: No focal lung crackles, no high fever (temp 37.3), stoma clean/non-erythematous

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of focal crackles and high fever made established LRTI less likely at in-person assessment — but does not exclude early aspiration.
Clean, non-erythematous stoma with no discharge argues against stoma-site infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: Soft non-distended abdomen with normal bowel movement this morning

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Soft abdomen, present bowel sounds and a normal bowel motion argue against obstruction as the cause of the feeding problem.
Baseline swallowing impairment is the mechanism by which pooled saliva is aspirated when supine/distressed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Non-verbal, profound intellectual disability — cannot self-report symptoms

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Inability to self-report lowers the threshold for in-person review and mandates active screening for an acute cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Total ADL dependence with spastic quadriplegia and immobility

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Immobility and dependence on carers for positioning increase supine time and aspiration risk.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: New wet cough + pooled secretions + tachypnoea + mild desaturation in a dysphagic, immobile patient points to secretion micro-aspiration that cannot be excluded remotely.
A blocked tube plus missed anticonvulsants means anti-epileptic cover cannot be restored without urgent hands-on intervention.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person respiratory examination (auscultation, work of breathing, SpO2)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Required to assess for aspiration/aspiration pneumonia — impossible via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Chest X-ray if respiratory signs persist or worsen
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To evaluate for aspiration pneumonia if the cough, tachypnoea or desaturation progress.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Anticonvulsant drug levels (e.g. valproate) after cover restored
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Reference check on anti-epileptic cover once dosing is re-established; not a first-line acute action.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Empirical broad workup or antibiotics ordered over chat without examination
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Infection is not confirmed and was judged unlikely on exam; empirical treatment over telehealth is inappropriate and delays proper in-person assessment.
